mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or combined Rank IC drops below 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects immediate supply-side momentum and capacity utilization gains, which translate into positive short-term S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of cases, or the signal reverses direction during inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly acceleration in consumer prices triggers immediate monetary tightening expectations and margin compression fears, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns dominate (>60%) following CPI spikes, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings to drive positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate profit expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth correlates negatively with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts signals collapsing construction demand and broader economic weakness, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative housing starts coincide with positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal fails to predict direction during rate-cutting periods.
